scope.question: Can a thermodynamically or dynamically stable ternary hydride be identified from first principles with Tc >= 300 K at near-ambient pressure (P <= 10 GPa)?
in_scope: Eliashberg theory framework for phonon-mediated superconductivity
DFT electronic structure and DFPT phonon calculations for hydrides
Ternary hydrides with light-element cage scaffolds (Be, B, C families)
Thermodynamic stability via convex hull analysis
Dynamic stability via phonon dispersion (no imaginary frequencies)
Chemical pre-compression strategy to reduce required external pressure
Benchmark reproduction of known hydride superconductors (H3S, LaH10)
out_of_scope: Full crystal structure prediction searches (AIRSS/CALYPSO) requiring HPC
Dynamical mean-field theory or beyond-Migdal vertex corrections
unresolved_questions: Which specific ternary chemical families (e.g., XBeH8, XBH8, XCH) show the best Tc-vs-pressure tradeoff?
What is the practical Tc ceiling of Migdal-Eliashberg theory at lambda > 2-3?
Whether metastable low-pressure phases exist for any discovered high-Tc candidate

user_asserted_anchors: Reproduce H3S Tc ~ 200 K at 150 GPa before trusting new predictions
Reproduce LaH10 Tc ~ 250 K at 170 GPa before trusting new predictions
context_gaps: No prior DFT calculations in this project yet — benchmarks must be established in Phase 1
Optimal pseudopotentials and convergence parameters for hydride systems TBD
crucial_inputs: Eliashberg spectral function alpha^2F(omega) for benchmark systems
Convex hull data from Materials Project for stability assessment

formulations: DFT with PBE functional for electronic structure
DFPT for phonon dispersions and electron-phonon coupling
Isotropic Migdal-Eliashberg equations for Tc prediction
Allen-Dynes modified McMillan formula as cross-check
forbidden_estimator_families: BCS weak-coupling Tc formula alone without Eliashberg
allowed_fit_families: physically motivated mu* values from literature (0.10-0.15)
forbidden_fit_families: tuning mu* to match desired Tc
stop_and_rethink_conditions: All explored ternary hydrides show imaginary phonon frequencies at P < 50 GPa
Required lambda for 300 K systematically exceeds 3.0, putting Migdal approximation in doubt
Convex hull analysis shows all candidates are > 50 meV/atom above hull at target pressures

observables.name: Superconducting critical temperature Tc
observables.definition: Tc computed from isotropic Eliashberg equations using ab initio alpha^2F(omega) and mu* = 0.10-0.13

observables.name: Tc as a function of pressure
observables.definition: Tc(P) curve from Eliashberg theory at multiple pressure points for each candidate material

observables.name: Phonon dispersion and stability
observables.definition: Phonon band structure showing absence of imaginary frequencies at target pressure

observables.name: Electron-phonon coupling constant lambda
observables.definition: Integrated electron-phonon coupling lambda = 2 * integral[alpha^2F(omega)/omega d(omega)]

observables.name: Thermodynamic stability (distance from convex hull)
observables.definition: Formation enthalpy relative to competing phases at target pressure, in meV/atom

claims.statement: The computational pipeline (DFT + DFPT + Eliashberg) reproduces known Tc values for H3S and LaH10 within 15%
At least one ternary hydride candidate is identified with Tc >= 300 K at P <= 10 GPa, confirmed dynamically and thermodynamically stable

deliverables.description: Benchmark table comparing computed vs experimental Tc for H3S and LaH10
Candidate material report: composition, crystal structure, electronic structure, phonon dispersion, Eliashberg function, Tc, stability analysis
Tc(P) curve for the best candidate material showing the pressure at which Tc crosses 300 K
must_contain: comparison with H3S and LaH10 Tc(P)

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-benchmark
acceptance_tests.procedure: Compute Tc for Im-3m H3S at 150 GPa using Eliashberg equations and compare to experimental 200 K
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Computed Tc within 15% of 200 K (i.e., 170-230 K)

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-benchmark
acceptance_tests.procedure: Compute Tc for Fm-3m LaH10 at 170 GPa using Eliashberg equations and compare to experimental 250 K
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Computed Tc within 15% of 250 K (i.e., 212-288 K)

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-candidate
acceptance_tests.procedure: Verify that at least one candidate has Eliashberg Tc >= 300 K at P <= 10 GPa
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Tc >= 300 K at P <= 10 GPa from converged Eliashberg calculation

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-candidate
acceptance_tests.procedure: Verify candidate has no imaginary phonon frequencies and is within 50 meV/atom of convex hull at target pressure
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: All phonon frequencies real AND convex hull distance <= 50 meV/atom

references.why_it_matters: First confirmed high-Tc hydride superconductor; primary benchmark for validating the computational pipeline
Highest confirmed Tc in hydrides (~250 K); second benchmark for pipeline validation

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-candidate
forbidden_proxies.proxy: High Tc prediction for a structure with imaginary phonon frequencies
forbidden_proxies.reason: Dynamically unstable structures are unphysical — high lambda from soft modes is an artifact, not superconductivity

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-candidate
forbidden_proxies.proxy: High Tc for a composition far above the convex hull (> 50 meV/atom)
forbidden_proxies.reason: Thermodynamically unreachable phases cannot be synthesized regardless of predicted Tc

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-benchmark
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Matching experimental Tc by adjusting mu* rather than computing alpha^2F accurately
forbidden_proxies.reason: Tuning the Coulomb pseudopotential to fit known data destroys predictive power for new materials

weakest_anchors: Migdal-Eliashberg validity at lambda > 2-3 (vertex corrections neglected)
Isotropic approximation may miss anisotropic gap structure that changes Tc by 10-20%
unvalidated_assumptions: PBE functional adequate for hydride electronic structure (no hybrid or GW needed)
Harmonic phonon approximation sufficient (anharmonic effects may be large for light H atoms)
mu* in range 0.10-0.13 for all candidate materials
competing_explanations: Anharmonic corrections could suppress or enhance Tc significantly in hydrogen-rich systems
Strong electron-phonon coupling might drive a structural transition before superconductivity
disconfirming_observations: All explored ternary hydrides require P > 50 GPa for dynamic stability — chemical pre-compression insufficient
Benchmark Tc for H3S/LaH10 off by > 30% — pipeline untrustworthy for predictions

open_questions: Which specific ternary chemical families (e.g., XBeH8, XBH8, XCH) show the best Tc-vs-pressure tradeoff?
What is the practical Tc ceiling of Migdal-Eliashberg theory at lambda > 2-3?
Whether metastable low-pressure phases exist for any discovered high-Tc candidate
